title: Senior Product Manager
description: Impact As our Senior Product Manager for Marketplace Consumer Experience, you sit at the center of Shipt’s three-sided marketplace, owning the digital journey from "App Open" to "Order Placed" for millions of users. You are responsible for balancing the complex needs of customers, retail partners, and personal shoppers to drive measurable growth in conversion, retention, and lifetime value. Every day, you will collaborate with Engineering, Design, and Data Science teams using tools like Amplitude, Jira, and Slack to identify friction points and launch high-impact experiments. You will act as the primary architect of the product roadmap for our core eCommerce and retail media agents, translating deep user research into features that make every transaction feel like magic. Senior Product Managers in this role are asked to partner closely with cross-functional teams to identify and deliver robust solutions that move the needle for our users. You will be tasked for driving synergies between the in-app and off-app user experiences. Experience with marketing teams and marketing tech stacks is a plus. What You’ll Need to Be Successful 5+ years of Product Management experience specifically launching high-traffic features within B2C e-commerce or multi-sided marketplaces and demonstrated partnership with marketing stakeholders. Proven autonomy in managing the full product lifecycle—from initial discovery and vision setting to post-launch optimization—without requiring daily tactical guidance.

title: Senior Product Manager
description: What You’ll Do: Define the Vision: Own the product vision and roadmap for core consumer-facing eCommerce agents and internal agents related to retail media network, merch, marketing, and fulfillment operations, aligning with Shipt’s long-term marketplace strategy. Drive Growth: Identify friction points in the user journey and leverage data to launch experiments that improve conversion, retention, and lifetime value. Master the Marketplace: Navigate the nuances of three-sided marketplace dynamics (Customer, Shopper, Retailer) Collaborate Cross-Functionally: Work closely with Engineering, Design, Data Science, and Marketing to move from ideas to high-impact product launches. Be the Voice of the Customer: Deep-dive into user research and feedback to build features that solve real pain points for our diverse member base. A Strategic Thinker: You don't just build features; you solve problems. You understand how a change in the consumer app ripples through the entire delivery ecosystem. Data-Informed, Not Just Data-Driven: You are comfortable with data analysis and A/B testing, but you also have the intuition to know when to trust your gut and move fast. An Expert Communicator: You can distill technical complexity into a compelling narrative for stakeholders and executives. Resilient & Scrappy: You thrive in a fast-paced environment and aren't afraid to pivot when the data tells a new story.

title: Senior Engineering Manager – Fullstack Mobile
description: Impact The Senior Engineering Manager – Fullstack Mobile role is a senior people and technology leader within Shipt’s Gig Operations engineering organization. This role reports directly to the Director of Engineering for Gig Ops Fulfillment and is responsible for leading 10+ engineers across mobile frontend and backend disciplines. This leader oversees one or more agile software development squads, driving long-term technical strategy, engineering excellence, delivery predictability, and team health across diverse experiences and capabilities. Compared to the Engineering Manager role, the Senior Engineering Manager operates with broader ownership, deeper architectural influence, higher cross-functional visibility, a wider breadth of stakeholders, and increased responsibility for scaling teams, managing complex initiatives, and shaping engineering culture. Senior Engineering Managers collaborate with Staff & Principal Engineers, Product Managers, UX Designers & Managers, Data Scientists, Operations Managers, and partner engineering teams to execute against multi-quarter roadmaps, strengthen engineering fundamentals, develop talent to meet engineering expectations of the next level, remove organizational bottlenecks, and guide engineers through ambiguity with clarity and strategic direction. This is a high-impact role that requires strong technical depth, exceptional leadership maturity, strategic decision-making, and the ability to steward tech assets and engineering teams through modernization and AI-driven transformation.

title: Staff Application Security Engineer
description: Impact As a Staff Engineer on our Application Security team, you will be instrumental in leading and driving secure practices across Shipt. You’ll be responsible for development practices across our engineering organization and building software systems to make secure development easier. In this role, you will design, implement, and scale security controls and processes that protect Shipt’s users, empower Shipt developers, ensure the safety of our applications, and protect user data. As a Staff Engineer, you are expected to operate at a high level of technical proficiency, provide technical leadership, mentor other team members, and influence security culture at all levels of the company. If you are enthusiastic about cybersecurity, enjoy sharing your knowledge, and thrive on personal and professional growth, you're exactly the kind of engineer we're looking for. Our Shipt Cybersecurity team is growing, and we're looking for talented individuals who can work both independently and collaboratively, with a passion for mentoring and developing their colleagues. You will gain valuable experience collaborating with cross-disciplinary teams, contributing to the protection of customers and shoppers nationwide What You’ll Need to Be Successful You have extensive software engineering experience with focus on developer tooling or infrastructure You have strong programming skills in Go, Python, and/or JavaScript/TypeScript

title: Principal Engineer
description: Impact As a Principal Software Engineer on the Developer Operations team, your primary role will be to architect, influence, and build strategic infrastructure and delivery systems and tools. You will be a technical leader within the Developer and Ops Enablement engineering organization and partner closely with engineering and product leads across multiple teams. Your work will consist of a balance between technical guidance, architectural design, and implementation to deliver high quality software solutions in a rapidly evolving e-commerce environment. What You’ll Need to Be Successful 6+ years of software engineering experience with progressive responsibilities and operating applications at scale Working expertise in building, debugging, deploying, and scaling applications built on various technology stacks 3+ years of experience with modern cloud platform providers, including but not limited to AWS, GCP, and Azure Working expertise with containerization - Kubernetes, Docker Working expertise with observability tools and technologies, such as time series databases, log aggregation, metric visualization, distributed tracing, APM Pay Range: $140,000- $217,000 Please note that the salary range above is a guideline, and individual total compensation will vary based on factors such as qualifications, skill level, competencies, and work location. Skills & Education This list includes key skills used in this job but is not inclusive of all skills needed for the role. Please see any required education below.

title: Senior Engineer
description: Impact As a Senior Software Engineer on our Identity & Access Management (IAM) team, you’ll design and own mission‑critical authentication and authorization services that keep Shipt secure and seamless. You’ll build and evolve secure, scalable IAM capabilities—SSO, MFA, token services, policy, and role management—integrating platforms like Auth0 and Okta with our applications and API Gateway. You’ll collaborate closely with Security, Infrastructure, and Product engineering teams to drive best practices, reliability, and a great developer experience. You’ll also set the bar for code quality and operational excellence through clear designs, thoughtful documentation, and hands‑on mentorship What You’ll Need to Be Successful 4+ years of software development experience delivering and operating production services. Bachelors degree or equivalent experience required Ownership of mission‑critical systems, including support for incident response and production troubleshooting. Strong grasp of REST principles and API design; experience with service‑oriented/microservice architectures and distributed systems. Practical expertise in IAM: designing authn/authz flows; token‑based auth (OAuth/JWT); SSO and MFA; secure session and policy/role management. Hands‑on with identity platforms such as Auth0 and/or Okta, integrating identity with diverse applications and services. Proficiency with modern stacks and tooling, e.g., Go, JavaScript, Terraform, and operational tooling for deploy/monitoring.
